notes: Consumer health website; secondary source only; no original research; replaced with peer-reviewed meta-analyses
Meta-analysis of 25 RCTs showing ACV reduces fasting blood sugar by ~21 mg/dL and HbA1c by 0.91% in T2D patients; added to resource
RCT in T2D patients with dyslipidemia showing improvements in glycemic indices (HOMA-IR, HOMA-B, QUICKI) and oxidative stress after 8 weeks; added to resource
2025 GRADE systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis on ACV glycemic control in T2D; confirms ~22 mg/dL FBG reduction and dose-dependent effects; Frontiers in Nutrition
2025 MDPI Nutrients systematic review of 10 RCTs on ACV body composition; significant reductions in body weight and waist circumference; moderate certainty
